Video.summary: In this episode Andy and his friend Scotty take on Kodiak Island, Alaska for an adventure of Salmon fishing and hunting black tail deer. Whilst traversing steep mountains in the company of Kodiak brown bears and taking on cold Alaskan waters, Andy presents useful information for fellow outdoor enthusiasts all whilst building friendships with the great people of Kodiak Big Timber Lodge.
Video.title: Wildbound - "Alaska"

Video.summary: In this episode Andy goes back to his home farm in Alabama for deer and quail hunting. Not only does he make incredible dishes with his former ‘masterchef’ contestant Alex which he serves to local children in need but he explains how hunting is never about “just the hunt” but about the camaraderie, friendship and memories made through the pursuit of the harvest.
Video.title: Wildbound - "Alabama"

Video.summary: The story of Team Winchester shooters Zach Kienbaum and Desirae Edmunds is told. Zach and Desi discuss how they got started in the shooting sports and how important coaching has become to both of them. They also talk shooting tips. Theo Ribbs breaks down the shotgun sports while Chris Keefer, Casey Keefer, Paul Sawyer, Nikki Boxler and Gabe Adair have a friendly sporting clays competition.
Video.title: “Clays and Competition”

Video.summary: The Pacific Northwest does not have the easiest conditions to train in for this shooting sports team. For this Seattle-based SST Ravens, this skeet and trap team finds that the challenging weather they train in comes in handy when competing. Also: Competition shooting tips form pro shooter Theo Ribbs.
Video.title: “Ink in the Sky”

Video.summary: Rusty Creasey joins the Spring Thunder crew for an incredible turkey hunt in Oklahoma. The guys are blessed to fill 6 tags in a short time and have several memorable hunts.
Video.title: Spring Thunder - "Oklahoma"

Video.summary: Justin and Brandi with Big Dreams Outdoors finish up their slam hunting Merriam turkeys with 7J Outfitters in Wyoming. It was a tough week for Justin but persistence pays off!
Video.title: Spring Thunder - "Big Merriam Dreams"

Video.summary: Melissa Bachman has made her name over the last two decades as one of the most dedicated hunters and producers in the outdoor industry. In this episode we’ll tag along with Melissa while she chases big game around the world with her Winchester in hand. First we’ll head to Colorado as Melissa spot and stalks giant mule deer, then we’ll head across the pond to New Zealand to chase mountain ibex and tar, then head back to the states for Montana whitetail at ground level.
Video.title: “Big Game Bachman”

Video.summary: If you had a blank check to build the best fishing boat possible, what would you create? Capt. Justin gets his wish, but it doesn’t come quickly or easily.
Video.title: "Dream Boat Build"

Video.summary: Capt. Justin takes a fellow guide on his new boat to see how it measures up. Using only public waypoints on his Humminbird unit, they find exploring to be as much fun as it is fruitful.
Video.title: "Fishing the Dream Boat"

Video.summary: Huckberry's flagship adventure series is back in Japan and the DIRT crew is hungry as ever for adventure. This time, they’re diving into the Kansai region—where millennia-old traditions flow from the shore of Lake Biwa to surf breaks on the coast. Chef Coleman Griffin gives them a taste of the deep cultural immersion that inspires his culinary practice, from chef knives descended from samurai swords to fermented carp that went on to spawn sushi. And turns out, the perfect meal does exist, and the crew knew it as soon as they had a bite of their final omakase.
Video.title: DIRT - "Japanese Sushi Origins, Samurai Steel & Rich Culinary Traditions"

Image.alt: DIRT - "Japanese Sushi Origins, Samurai Steel & Rich Culinary Traditions"
DIRT - "Japanese Sushi Origins, Samurai Steel & Rich Culinary Traditions"
DIRT - "Japanese Sushi Origins, Samurai Steel & Rich Culinary Traditions"

Video.summary: Capt. Scott Walker and Capt. Steve Rodger meet up with Capt. Brain Wilson in Galveston, TX for a taste of what the Texas Gulf Coast has to offer.
Video.title: "Welcome to Galveston"

Video.summary: Capt. Scott Walker and Capt. Steve Rodger join Capt. Brain Wilson in Galveston, TX for some hot Red Snapper action.
Video.title: "Red Snapper Slam"

Video.summary: Capt. Scott Walker and Capt. Steve Rodger go north for a change to sight fish tripletails in Florida Bay.
Video.title: "Triple Tail in the Florida Bay"

Video.summary: Capt. Scott Walker and Capt. Steve Rodger head to the hump on a windy day to target tunas and find some bonus mahis and tripletails as well.
Video.title: "Rough Day at the Hump"

Video.summary: Being consistently productive on the water means knowing how to adapt to conditions. Capt. Scott Walker and Capt. Steve Rodger switch it up and fish shallow for sailfish.
Video.title: "Sailfish Save The Day"

Video.summary: Capt. Scott Walker and Capt. Steve Rodger launch from the incomparable Oceans Edge Resort in Key West, Florida to hunt down all kinds of sport fish.
Video.title: "Key West Mahi and Sails"

Video.summary: U.S. Army veteran Domingo Martinez recounts overcoming the life altering effects of trauma he received as a child and on the DMZ.
Video.title: "Domingo Martinez"

Video.summary: Part 2, Day 2; Capt. Danny is still up in Virginia Beach. Yesterday’s mission was giant red drum, today’s mission is to hit the beginning of the Chesapeake Bay’s legendary cobia migration.
Video.title: "Virginia Beach Brawlers Pt 2"

Video.summary: During this episode of Ship Shape TV Essentials the SSTV team does a - deep dive into a product founded by Engineered Marine Coatings called the Genius Bucket. The Genius Bucket being the ultimate DIY paint kit for those looking to tackle a marine painting job on there own. The team also checks out the capabilities it takes to restoring the older metal rub rail which derived from the Glastron project.
Video.title: Essentials - "Rub Rail Restoration & DIY Paint Bucket"

Image.alt: Essentials - "Rub Rail Restoration & DIY Paint Bucket"
Essentials - "Rub Rail Restoration & DIY Paint Bucket"
Essentials - "Rub Rail Restoration & DIY Paint Bucket"

Video.summary: The tide is right for Capt. Jonathan Moss to chase that Northeast Florida slam.
Video.title: "First Coast Fishing"

Video.summary: Mark Peterson and Earl Peterson are headed down to Kentucky for the opening of deer season. With this being the earliest that a bowhunter can hunt whitetails, it can result in a giant velvet whitetail harvest. After a fun night enjoying the Swon Brothers' music, it's time to dial the bow in, freshen up the Buck Bourbon at feed sites and hope that Brows or Kevins' buck show themselves. How will the story end on the Giant Deer named Brows?
Video.title: "2022 Kentucky Whitetail Velvet Opener"

Video.summary: Down in Sebastion, the monster snook wait. These fish are so elusive, men have gotten divorced chasing these things. These can be once in a lifetime fish, and even then it's not a guarantee.
Video.title: "Hunting a Mogan"

Video.summary: Michael Pitts of Realtree Road Trips joins Anthony Virga and Matt Garger hunting the hills of New York.
Video.title: Spring Thunder - "Pitts Hunts New York"

Video.summary: The 2024 season of Spring Thunder is officially underway. David Blanton kicks off the season in southern Florida by hunting the beautiful Williams Farms with Southern Outdoors Outfitters. The action is hot and it doesn’t take long to get on some angry longbeards.
Video.title: Spring Thunder - "Gobblers Looking for a Fight | South-Florida Strutters | Spring Thunder"

Image.alt: Spring Thunder - "Gobblers Looking for a Fight | South-Florida Strutters | Spring Thunder"
Spring Thunder - "Gobblers Looking for a Fight | South-Florida Strutters | Spring Thunder"
Spring Thunder - "Gobblers Looking for a Fight | South-Florida Strutters | Spring Thunder"

Video.summary: What starts as a normal trip turns wild as Capt. Jared and Capt. Garrett get swarmed by Cobia.
Video.title: "The Prodigal Son"

Video.summary: On this episode, we travel to Missouri River near Chamberlain, SD and connect with the South Dakota chapter of Paralyzed Veterans of America for the Joel Niemeyer "Gone Fishing" event. We get to guide and share the story of 100 year old WW2 Veteran Kenneth Carlson and his family. We also honor our next Rochester, MN Scheels Hometown Hero, Dustin Draeger!
Video.title: "Paralyzed Veterans Of America"

Video.summary: Capt. Jared and Father-Son duo Captain’s Hunter & Chris Barron have Sailfish in their sights.
Video.title: "Like Father Like Son"

Video.summary: Meet just a few of the amazing crew members of the Old Salt Board of Directors. This nonprofit is more than just a fishing group. They make a difference in their community.
Video.title: "Old Salts"

Video.summary: The Southards continue their family tradition on Table Rock Lake. While they’re there, Trenton spends a day with “Mr. Worldwide” James Watson and learns some things from one of the best anglers on the lake.
Video.title: "Table Rock Worldwide"

Video.summary: Nick hits the Islamorada backwaters with Bud N' Mary's legends Capts. Richard and Nick Stancyzk to tempt bonefish and permit on the flats.
Video.title: "Islamorada Bones and Permit"

Video.summary: Day 1 of the Baitfuel Fishing Tour Championship starts at Joel Stone Heritage Park in Gananoque, Ontario on the mighty St. Lawrence River and Lake Ontario.
Video.title: BaitFuel Fishing Tour - "Tour Championship Day 1"

Video.summary: Every spring brings a new opportunity to take a step forward & recall the memories of seasons past. World War II Veteran and retired Judge Arden Hill has spent most of his life chasing turkeys in the hills of Tennessee. Now in his 90’s, Arden doesn’t let age stand in the way of his love for spring turkey hunting. Also: patterning turkey shotguns and hunting spring gobblers.
Video.title: “The Veteran”

Video.summary: Winchester introduces the Shoot United brand aimed at offering useful information and tips to the millions of new shooters entering the shooting sports. New York native and Midland University competitive shotgun shooter Amy Cawley and her journey as a student-athlete is followed. Also: Theo Ribbs gives shooting tips for competition and Foster Bartholow reviews Winchester AA Diamond Grade.
Video.title: "Let’s Shoot United"

Video.summary: Charles Rodney’s relationship with his hunting dogs, passion for field-to-table living and connection to the land make his love for chasing rabbits contagious while the heritage of Winchester’s small game ammunition makes this beloved pastime accessible for generations to come. Also: a Model 42 collection for the ages and hunting Kansas coyotes with the Keefer Brothers.
Video.title: "The Rabbit Hunter"

Video.summary: Deer season — the sights, the sounds and the smells stick with you. It’s about remembering those who came before us and setting the right example for those who look up to us. It’s about protecting the tradition, so they always have something to look forward to. When communities come together, we see the real reason for this special time of year.
Video.title: “Deer Season”

Video.summary: John Sewell has played a huge part in the development of feeding Fortified Cottonseed 365 days a year. Laramie Barrett of Fortified Cottonseed joins John for a great weekend of hunting at his ranch in Uvalde, Texas. This is a great time for Laramie to get away from the office and see the first hand of how their product is performing.
Video.title: "Fortified Cottonseed"

Video.summary: Each year we get to spend time in camp with some of our best friends. Zach from Big & J gets some time in a blind while reflecting on what hunting in Texas means to him. Texas provides a wide variety of game, and chasing these creatures all across the state is some of the best times had.
Video.title: "Valued Partners"

Video.summary: Capt Brandon visits Quails Landing Plantation in South Georgia and his buddy Steven Redrick to do all things outdoors.
Video.title: "Quails Landing"

Video.summary: Randy is at the Raven in Missouri trying to take advantage of some dry weather and get some Big Tine borderline planted and check off a long list of other summer time prep work as opening day of deer season will be here before we know it.
Video.title: "Summer Time Work On The Raven"

Video.summary: David Blanton heads to southern Georgia to kick off the 2023 Georgia youth turkey season. The turkeys are fired up, but so is Addox Hollingsworth.
Video.title: "Strutters At 10 Yards | First Georgia Gobbler Of The Year Down"

Image.alt: "Strutters At 10 Yards | First Georgia Gobbler Of The Year Down"
"Strutters At 10 Yards | First Georgia Gobbler Of The Year Down"
"Strutters At 10 Yards | First Georgia Gobbler Of The Year Down"

Video.summary: JP heads to Tropic Star Lodge to learn slow pitch jigging with Benny Ortiz and Capt. Blaine Anderson.
Video.title: "Slow Pitch Jigging"

Video.summary: Young Captain Reed Tudor hosts Captain Mike in Duck Key, FL and puts him on Micro Tarpon, Redfish and Goliath Grouper.
Video.title: "Anything and Everything"

Video.summary: Capt Josh and Johnny from Johnny Jigs head out with gorgeous weather on their side to hit some reefs and put the jigs to work!
Video.title: "Johnny Jigs"

Video.summary: Kyle explores the Great Lakes on his Sea-Doo in search of walleye and lake trout.
Video.title: "Great Lakes Extreme Fishing"

Video.summary: Ty has been bow hunting turkey's for quite some time. Every year he tries to focus on getting all of his families tags filled before trying to harvest a gobbler himself. This year was no different but it ended off with a perfect hunt for Ty killing a big mature double bearded eastern gobbler!
Video.title: "Ty's Double Bearded Gobbler! Perfect Bow Shot!"

Image.alt: "Ty's Double Bearded Gobbler! Perfect Bow Shot!"
"Ty's Double Bearded Gobbler! Perfect Bow Shot!"
"Ty's Double Bearded Gobbler! Perfect Bow Shot!"

Video.summary: We follow along with the crew from Struttinbuck on their annual Father Son hunt at FE Hill. No matter what happens throughout the year this is always a must have trip and a time to settle down and enjoy the great outdoors.
Video.title: "Father and Son Hunt"

Video.summary: If you’ve ever heard, seen, or dreamed of the term van life, there is one man to thank for that, and his name is Foster Huntington. The filmmaker, photographer, and author has a storied career that includes the legendary book, "Home is Where You Park it". We met up with Foster (out on the road naturally) and he gave us a tour of his personal off-road camping rig he takes around the country.
Video.title: "How this Van Life Pioneer Set Up His Perfect Off-Grid Camper | Foster Huntington"

Image.alt: "How this Van Life Pioneer Set Up His Perfect Off-Grid Camper | Foster Huntington"
"How this Van Life Pioneer Set Up His Perfect Off-Grid Camper | Foster Huntington"
"How this Van Life Pioneer Set Up His Perfect Off-Grid Camper | Foster Huntington"

Video.summary: Tyler and Colton Jordan go after the eastern bird in the quest for Colton's first ever Grand Slam. David Blanton also hunts opening day and has 6 gobblers come into the decoy.
Video.title: Spring Thunder - "Georgia Opener"

Video.summary: We join Blake Barnett alongside hunter Jimmy Reynolds as they take part in the 2nd Annual Pierce Dutton Memorial Hunt. This meaningful whitetail hunt is made possible by the great folks at Disabled Outdoorsmen. Jimmy settles in at the G2 Ranch in South Texas, where prime whitetail country and a shared passion for the outdoors set the stage for an unforgettable hunt.
Video.title: "Disabled Outdoorsmen"

Video.summary: The highs and lows of fishing teach us to ride the wave, and the captains, mates, and everyday Costa Rican citizens have a phrase for such a mindset – “Pura Vida.” Those two words are more of a cultural ethos than a casual expression, and a reminder to remain smiling, thankful, and to never sweat the small stuff. - For the crews and anglers of Los Sueńos, the good days far outweigh the bad. Nestled inside a small nook of Playa Herradura, Costa Rica’s Los Sueńos Resort and Marina serves as a luxury embarkation point for some of the world’s most heralded bluewater fishing. Many places tout similar claims, but numbers don’t lie: It is common for a single boat to release over a dozen marlin in a single day, and the area holds perhaps the largest concentrations of big Pacific sailfish anywhere.
Video.title: "La Pesca Pura Vida"

Video.summary: Randy and Cody are back on the Raven in Missouri and with opening day of bow season fast approaching they are scrambling to get all the final prep work done and correct any mistakes they might have made along the way.
Video.title: "Brotherly Love"

Video.summary: A trip to south TX to hunt the Wexford ranch has become a tradition for us over the past few years.
Video.title: Behind The Draw - "Texas Tradition"
